newsSinceJun6: May 2026 monthly revenue released: NT$1.307B, +3.03% MoM and +1.15% YoY (first modest YoY uptick), but Jan-May cumulative revenue still -2.9% YoY. Consumer/PC-centric interface IC demand remains soft per Q1 call and Morgan Stanley's gross-margin-miss note; Q2 guide of US$127-141M (GM 40-44%) unchanged. No new contracts, design wins, M&A, or capacity news. Today's +4.2% move appears flow-driven (TPEx tech rally), not fundamental.

sources.label: SinoTrade - Parade-KY Q1 flat, Q2 revenue guided US$127-141M, GM 40-44%
Vocus - Morgan Stanley flags Q1 gross margin miss, weak consumer demand

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already cut it 10 ranks for indirect AI exposure after the YTD run; today's +4.2% came on flow, not fundamentals, and the flat May revenue print (-2.9% YoY cumulative) neither strengthens nor breaks the selective-exposure thesis, so rank 39 stands with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: PCIe/USB4 retimer and high-speed interface content gives genuine optionality on AI server and edge-device attach, and May revenue turning slightly positive YoY hints the consumer trough is in.
verdicts.neutral: Business remains dominated by PC/display Tcon and touch ICs with Q2 guided roughly flat (US$127-141M, GM 40-44%); AI-adjacent revenue is too small to make Parade a bottleneck name, justifying its low-30s/high-30s rank.
verdicts.bear: Consumer interface demand stays soft, gross margin keeps compressing (MS flagged the Q1 miss), and the +19% YTD rerating on flow leaves the stock exposed if TPEx momentum reverses with no AI catalyst to backstop it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of meaningful PCIe Gen6 retimer or AI-server interface design wins, or June/Q2 revenue beating the US$127-141M guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Prolonged consumer/PC weakness and margin compression while the AI narrative fails to translate into revenue, unwinding the flow-driven rerating.
